main_paper.raw: {"methods":["Re-implementation of Neighbourhood Components Analysis (NCA) using SGD instead of L-BFGS","Extension of NCA to learn linear projections into higher dimensions (not dimensionality reduction)","Use of soft nearest neighbor inference rule","Replacement of linear projection in NCA with a neural network for nonlinear embeddings","Stochastic Neighborhood Sampling (SNS) for efficient and generalizable training","Pre-defined feature transform on numerical tabular entries","MODERNNCA: the final deep NCA implementation combining the above techniques"],"problems":["Improving the performance of deep learning methods on tabular data","Bridging the gap between deep tabular models and traditional machine learning methods (e.g., GBDT) in terms of accuracy","Revitalizing classical nearest neighbor methods for modern tabular data tasks using deep learning techniques"],"datasets":["300 tabular datasets (not individually named, but a large-scale benchmark)"],"metrics":["Average rank (across methods) on classification and regression tasks","Training time (seconds)","Memory usage","Accuracy"],"results":[{"metric":"Average rank (classification)","value":"1st (MODERNNCA ranked first among all methods)"},{"metric":"Average rank (regression)","value":"2nd (MODERNNCA just behind CatBoost)"},{"metric":"Training time","value":"Lower than other deep tabular models"},{"metric":"Memory usage","value":"Relatively lower than other deep tabular models"},{"metric":"Accuracy","value":"On par with CatBoost for regression, outperforms other deep and tree-based models"}],"novelty_claims":["Systematic revisiting and improvement of classical NCA for tabular data using modern deep learning techniques","Demonstration that a deep NCA approach (MODERNNCA) can match or outperform state-of-the-art tree-based and deep tabular models on a large benchmark","Introduction of stochastic neighborhood sampling (SNS) to improve both efficiency and generalizability in deep NCA","Empirical analysis revealing the impact of loss functions, prediction strategies, and deep architectures on NCA for tabular data","Providing a strong deep learning baseline for tabular data tasks, potentially revitalizing interest in nearest neighbor methods"]}

methods: Re-implementation of Neighbourhood Components Analysis (NCA) using SGD instead of L-BFGS
Extension of NCA to learn linear projections into higher dimensions (not dimensionality reduction)
Use of soft nearest neighbor inference rule
Replacement of linear projection in NCA with a neural network for nonlinear embeddings
Stochastic Neighborhood Sampling (SNS) for efficient and generalizable training
Pre-defined feature transform on numerical tabular entries
MODERNNCA: the final deep NCA implementation combining the above techniques
problems: Improving the performance of deep learning methods on tabular data
Bridging the gap between deep tabular models and traditional machine learning methods (e.g., GBDT) in terms of accuracy
Revitalizing classical nearest neighbor methods for modern tabular data tasks using deep learning techniques
datasets: 300 tabular datasets (not individually named, but a large-scale benchmark)
metrics: Average rank (across methods) on classification and regression tasks
Training time (seconds)
Memory usage
Accuracy

results.value: 1st (MODERNNCA ranked first among all methods)
2nd (MODERNNCA just behind CatBoost)
Lower than other deep tabular models
Relatively lower than other deep tabular models
On par with CatBoost for regression, outperforms other deep and tree-based models

novelty_claims: Systematic revisiting and improvement of classical NCA for tabular data using modern deep learning techniques
Demonstration that a deep NCA approach (MODERNNCA) can match or outperform state-of-the-art tree-based and deep tabular models on a large benchmark
Introduction of stochastic neighborhood sampling (SNS) to improve both efficiency and generalizability in deep NCA
Empirical analysis revealing the impact of loss functions, prediction strategies, and deep architectures on NCA for tabular data
Providing a strong deep learning baseline for tabular data tasks, potentially revitalizing interest in nearest neighbor methods

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["TabR: a feed-forward neural network with a custom k-Nearest-Neighbors-like retrieval component","Attention-like mechanism for retrieving and aggregating nearest neighbors' information"],"problems":["Improving the performance of deep learning models on tabular data tasks (classification, regression)","Bridging the performance gap between tabular deep learning models and gradient-boosted decision trees (GBDT)","Designing efficient and effective retrieval-augmented tabular deep learning models"],"datasets":["A set of public tabular data benchmarks (including datasets with up to several million objects)","Recently proposed 'GBDT-friendly' benchmark with middle-scale tasks (Grinsztajn et al., 2022)"],"metrics":["Average performance across benchmarks (likely accuracy or similar task-specific metrics)","State-of-the-art achievement on individual datasets (likely accuracy, AUC, or similar)"],"results":[{"metric":"Average performance among tabular DL models","value":"TabR demonstrates the best average performance"},{"metric":"State-of-the-art on several datasets","value":"TabR achieves new SOTA results"},{"metric":"Performance on 'GBDT-friendly' benchmark","value":"TabR outperforms GBDT models"}],"novelty_claims":["TabR is a simple yet powerful retrieval-augmented tabular deep learning model that outperforms previous tabular DL models and even GBDT on certain benchmarks.","TabR introduces an efficient attention-like mechanism for nearest neighbor retrieval and aggregation in tabular DL.","TabR is significantly more efficient and less complex than prior retrieval-based tabular DL models while achieving higher performance.","The work highlights previously overlooked design aspects of attention mechanisms in retrieval-based tabular models."]}

novelty_claims: TabR is a simple yet powerful retrieval-augmented tabular deep learning model that outperforms previous tabular DL models and even GBDT on certain benchmarks.
TabR introduces an efficient attention-like mechanism for nearest neighbor retrieval and aggregation in tabular DL.

The work highlights previously overlooked design aspects of attention mechanisms in retrieval-based tabular models.

datasets: 95 datasets curated by TabZilla from OpenML
metrics: Classification accuracy (implied by context and comparison to baselines)

results.value: LoCalPFN achieves new state-of-the-art results on the 95-dataset TabZilla benchmark, outperforming both neural and tuned tree-based models

novelty_claims: First to combine retrieval (kNN) and fine-tuning on top of a transformer-based in-context learner (TabPFN) for tabular data

Establish new state-of-the-art results on a large and diverse tabular benchmark, outperforming both neural and tree-based models

novelty_claims: First to show that well-regularized plain MLPs can significantly and substantially outperform both recent neural network architectures and strong traditional ML methods (such as XGBoost) on tabular datasets in a large-scale, fair experimental study.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Comprehensive evaluation of TabPFN v2 on over 300 tabular datasets","Analysis of randomized feature tokens for handling data heterogeneity","Leave-one-fold-out approach to transform TabPFN v2 into a feature extractor","Divide-and-conquer mechanism inspired by Chain-of-Thought prompting for scalable inference on large-scale, high-dimensional, and many-category tasks"],"problems":["Generalization of tabular foundation models across heterogeneous datasets","Understanding the mechanisms behind TabPFN v2's success in in-context learning","Extending TabPFN v2's applicability to large-scale, high-dimensional, and many-category tabular datasets","Improving model performance on small-scale tabular datasets with limited data"],"datasets":["Over 300 tabular datasets spanning various domains, scales, dimensionalities, and tasks (including references to Grinsztajn et al., 2022; McElfresh et al., 2023; Ye et al., 2024b; Rubachev et al., 2025)"],"metrics":["Accuracy"],"results":[{"metric":"Accuracy (small- to medium-scale datasets)","value":"TabPFN v2 consistently outperforms both tree-based models and state-of-the-art deep tabular models"},{"metric":"Accuracy (large-scale, high-dimensional, many-category datasets)","value":"TabPFN v2's performance diminishes, but divide-and-conquer strategies significantly improve accuracy in these regimes"}],"novelty_claims":["Comprehensive evaluation of TabPFN v2 on a large and diverse set of over 300 tabular datasets","Identification and analysis of randomized feature tokens as the key mechanism for handling data heterogeneity in TabPFN v2","Proposal of a leave-one-fold-out approach to use TabPFN v2 as a feature encoder, enabling new applications such as visualization and error diagnosis","Introduction of divide-and-conquer post-hoc strategies (inspired by Chain-of-Thought prompting and ECOC) to extend TabPFN v2's applicability to large-scale, high-dimensional, and many-category tasks without retraining"]}

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Large-scale empirical comparison of 19 algorithms (including various neural networks and GBDTs) on tabular data","Systematic hyperparameter optimization (up to 30 settings per algorithm)","Metafeature analysis to correlate dataset properties with algorithm performance","Release of the TabZilla Benchmark Suite (36 hardest datasets)","Open-sourcing codebase and raw results for reproducibility"],"problems":["Determining when neural networks outperform gradient-boosted decision trees (GBDTs) on tabular data","Identifying dataset properties that favor NNs or GBDTs","Assessing the importance of algorithm selection versus hyperparameter tuning for tabular data","Providing robust benchmarks and tools for tabular data research"],"datasets":["176 tabular datasets (including OpenML-CC18 suite and OpenML Benchmarking Suite)","TabZilla Benchmark Suite (36 hardest datasets selected from the 176)"],"metrics":["Average rank across datasets","Classification/regression accuracy (implied by context)","Performance difference between algorithms","Correlation of metafeatures with algorithm performance"],"results":[{"metric":"Best average performance (all datasets)","value":"TabPFN and CatBoost are the top-performing algorithms"},{"metric":"TabPFN performance (training set size 3000)","value":"Outperforms all other algorithms on average, even when randomly sampling 3000 datapoints"},{"metric":"Fraction of datasets where simple baseline or light GBDT tuning suffices","value":"Surprisingly high fraction; for roughly one-third of datasets, light tuning of CatBoost or ResNet increases performance more than choosing between NNs and GBDTs"},{"metric":"Metafeature correlation","value":"Dataset regularity predicts NNs outperforming GBDTs; GBDTs better on skewed/heavy-tailed features and larger datasets"}],"novelty_claims":["Largest tabular data analysis to date: 19 algorithms, 176 datasets, over 500,000 models trained","Demonstrates that the 'NN vs. GBDT' debate is overemphasized for most tabular datasets; algorithm selection often less important than hyperparameter tuning","Provides new insights into which dataset properties favor NNs or GBDTs via extensive metafeature analysis","Releases the TabZilla Benchmark Suite (36 hardest datasets) and the largest open-source codebase for tabular data algorithms and benchmarks","Shows that TabPFN, despite being limited to small training sets, outperforms all other algorithms on average"]}

Open-sourcing codebase and raw results for reproducibility
problems: Determining when neural networks outperform gradient-boosted decision trees (GBDTs) on tabular data
Identifying dataset properties that favor NNs or GBDTs
Assessing the importance of algorithm selection versus hyperparameter tuning for tabular data
Providing robust benchmarks and tools for tabular data research

metrics: Average rank across datasets

Performance difference between algorithms

results.value: TabPFN and CatBoost are the top-performing algorithms
Outperforms all other algorithms on average, even when randomly sampling 3000 datapoints

results.metric: Fraction of datasets where simple baseline or light GBDT tuning suffices
results.value: Surprisingly high fraction; for roughly one-third of datasets, light tuning of CatBoost or ResNet increases performance more than choosing between NNs and GBDTs
Dataset regularity predicts NNs outperforming GBDTs; GBDTs better on skewed/heavy-tailed features and larger datasets

novelty_claims: Largest tabular data analysis to date: 19 algorithms, 176 datasets, over 500,000 models trained
Demonstrates that the 'NN vs. GBDT' debate is overemphasized for most tabular datasets; algorithm selection often less important than hyperparameter tuning
Provides new insights into which dataset properties favor NNs or GBDTs via extensive metafeature analysis
Releases the TabZilla Benchmark Suite (36 hardest datasets) and the largest open-source codebase for tabular data algorithms and benchmarks
Shows that TabPFN, despite being limited to small training sets, outperforms all other algorithms on average
